chunk_summary: This section discusses bedside feeding compensations for children with neurodisability, emphasizing posture, pacing, bolus control, and cross-setting implementation, including school plans for safe feeding practices.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of posture and trunk support in improving swallow safety for children with neurodisability.
Learn practical pacing strategies, including bolus size control and observable feeding actions.
Develop cross-setting feeding plans, including school-based safety prescriptions and emergency response protocols.
key_facts: Proximal stability supports distal control: prioritize stable pelvis and neutral head-neck alignment during feeding.
Avoid supine feeding in children with reflux; keep them upright after meals as feasible.
Pacing strategies include offering small boluses, waiting for swallow completion, and scheduling pauses to protect breathing-swallow coordination.
Infant feeding flow should be adjusted based on respiratory stability rather than age-based packaging labels.
Cross-setting feeding plans should include IDDSI levels, supervision requirements, positioning guidelines, and emergency choking response plans.
Evidence-based supportive care measures are essential
reference_quote: Texture modification is only one component of swallow safety in neurodisability; many children gain as much benefit from better posture, pacing, and caregiver technique as from thickening.
document_key_topics: Posture and trunk support in feeding safety
